title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. glabrescens
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. glabrescens, also known as woolly gray cotton lavender or grey santolina, is a plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and North Africa. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. glabrescens include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, calm the mind, and support overall well-being.
